user_query:
I’m preparing a respectful, apolitical tribute campaign to honor Lech’s contribution to Polish politics and law around a few upcoming dates. Please build and run a small command‑line script that turns the local inputs into a compact content pack for social posts in Polish.

Inputs (must be read by the script):
- input/events.json
- input/quotes.jsonl

Deliverables (must be generated by running your script):
- scripts/make_posts.py (the CLI script you implement)
- outputs/posts.csv
- outputs/usage_report.json

Requirements:
1) The script at scripts/make_posts.py must read both input files and generate exactly two post variants per event: one short post for X (platform value: "X", max 280 characters) and one longer post for Facebook (platform value: "Facebook", max 600 characters). With the provided inputs there should be 6 total posts.
2) Each post must:
   - Be written in Polish and honor Lech’s legacy respectfully.
   - Include the event date in ISO format (YYYY-MM-DD) and at least one keyword from that event’s "keywords" list.
   - Include exactly one quote from input/quotes.jsonl, enclosed in Polish quotation marks „…”, and append the tag [quote_id] at the end of the post.
   - Include 2–3 Polish hashtags derived from the event’s themes/keywords; do not include spaces within hashtags.
3) Write outputs/posts.csv with these columns in order: event_id, date, platform, quote_id, hashtags, char_count, post_text. Ensure char_count reflects the length of post_text.
4) Write outputs/usage_report.json with:
   - total_posts (integer)
   - per_platform (object with keys "X" and "Facebook" mapping to counts)
   - per_quote (object mapping quote_id to how many times it was used)
   - per_event (object mapping event_id to an object with keys x_quote_id and facebook_quote_id)
   - input_checksums (object mapping each input file path to its SHA256 hex digest)
5) Run the script locally to produce the deliverables exactly at the specified paths. I will verify that:
   - posts.csv has 6 rows (2 per event) and adheres to the char limits.
   - Every post’s quote_id occurs in input/quotes.jsonl and the quoted text matches that quote.
   - Each post includes the event date and at least one of that event’s keywords.
   - usage_report.json accurately summarizes the generated posts and includes valid SHA256 checksums of the two input files.

Paths:
- Read from: input/events.json and input/quotes.jsonl
- Write to: outputs/posts.csv and outputs/usage_report.json
- Script location: scripts/make_posts.py

Please include brief comments in the script explaining how inputs are parsed and lengths counted, then run it to generate the outputs. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
